speaker: PERICLES
text: Most honored Cleon, I must needs be gone.

speaker: PERICLES
text: My twelve months are expired, and Tyrus stands

speaker: PERICLES
text: In a litigious peace. You and your lady

speaker: PERICLES
text: Take from my heart all thankfulness. The gods

speaker: PERICLES
text: Make up the rest upon you.

speaker: CLEON
text: Your shakes of fortune, though they haunt you mortally,

speaker: CLEON
text: Yet glance full wond’ringly on us.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: O, your sweet queen! That the strict Fates had pleased

speaker: DIONYZA
text: You had brought her hither to have blessed mine eyes with her!

speaker: PERICLES
text: We cannot but obey the powers above us.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Could I rage and roar as doth the sea

speaker: PERICLES
text: She lies in, yet the end must be as ’tis.

speaker: PERICLES
text: My gentle babe Marina,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Whom, for she was born at sea, I have named so,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Here I charge your charity withal,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Leaving her the infant of your care,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Beseeching you to give her princely training,

speaker: PERICLES
text: That she may be mannered as she is born.

speaker: CLEON
text: Fear not, my lord, but think

speaker: CLEON
text: Your Grace, that fed my country with your corn,

speaker: CLEON
text: For which the people’s prayers still fall upon you,

speaker: CLEON
text: Must in your child be thought on. If neglection

speaker: CLEON
text: Should therein make me vile, the common body,

speaker: CLEON
text: By you relieved, would force me to my duty.

speaker: CLEON
text: But if to that my nature need a spur,

speaker: CLEON
text: The gods revenge it upon me and mine,

speaker: CLEON
text: To the end of generation!

speaker: PERICLES
text: I believe you.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Your honor and your goodness teach me to ’t

speaker: PERICLES
text: Without your vows.—Till she be married, madam,

speaker: PERICLES
text: By bright Diana, whom we honor, all

speaker: PERICLES
text: Unscissored shall this hair of mine remain,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Though I show ill in ’t. So I take my leave.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Good madam, make me blessèd in your care

speaker: PERICLES
text: In bringing up my child.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I have one myself,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Who shall not be more dear to my respect

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Than yours, my lord.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Madam, my thanks and prayers.

speaker: CLEON
text: We’ll bring your Grace e’en to the edge o’ th’ shore,

speaker: CLEON
text: Then give you up to the maskèd Neptune

speaker: CLEON
text: And the gentlest winds of heaven.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I will embrace your offer.—Come, dearest madam.—

speaker: PERICLES
text: O, no tears, Lychorida, no tears!

speaker: PERICLES
text: Look to your little mistress, on whose grace

speaker: PERICLES
text: You may depend hereafter.—Come, my lord.
